name: Substack API
what_it_does: Substack — newsletter and subscription platform with an unofficial/limited API for post creation and subscriber management, primarily designed for human writers rather than programmatic agent publishing workflows.
use_cases: Agents drafting newsletter content for human review before sending to Substack subscriber lists
Content cross-posting — agents using Substack as one newsletter distribution channel alongside Beehiiv or ConvertKit
Subscriber data queries — agents reading subscriber counts and growth metrics for publication analytics
Post scheduling — agents creating posts at optimal times based on subscriber engagement patterns
Migration automation — agents bulk-importing content archives from other platforms into Substack
not_for: Fully automated publishing pipelines — Substack has no official API and limits programmatic access
Developers needing a proper REST API — use Beehiiv, Ghost, or ConvertKit which have proper developer APIs
High-volume programmatic publishing — Substack is designed for individual human writers, not agent-scale automation
best_when: You already have an established Substack audience and need limited automation for draft creation or data export — not for building new agent-driven publishing pipelines.
avoid_when: You need a proper REST API for newsletter automation — Beehiiv and Ghost provide far better developer-friendly APIs for programmatic newsletter management.

auth.notes: No official API. Unofficial access requires session cookies from web login. Substack has no public API key system or OAuth. Limited unofficial endpoints exist but are subject to change without notice.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited posts to free subscribers; Substack takes 10% of paid subscription revenue
pricing.paid_tiers: Substack Pro for high-traffic publishers (revenue advance programs); 10% platform fee on paid subs
pricing.notes: Free to publish. Substack takes 10% + Stripe processing fees (~2.9% + 30¢) from paid subscriptions. No API pricing since there's no official API.

performance.rate_limits: No official API limits documented; unofficial access may be throttled or blocked

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No official API documentation. Unofficial endpoints discovered through network inspection. Error responses are HTML pages or unstructured JSON. Completely unreliable for production agent workflows.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No official API — idempotency guarantees do not exist. Unofficial endpoints have no idempotency controls. Agents cannot reliably detect or prevent duplicate posts.
known_agent_gotchas: Substack has NO official public API — any programmatic access uses unofficial reverse-engineered endpoints
Session cookie auth breaks on password changes, logouts, and Substack-side session invalidation
Unofficial endpoints change without notice and have broken existing automation tools repeatedly
Substack TOS prohibits automated posting without authorization — risk of account suspension
No webhook support — agents cannot receive real-time events for subscriber actions or post events

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant for the platform. TLS enforced. But session cookie auth for unofficial API access is a security anti-pattern — cookies can be stolen, have broad account access, and are not designed for programmatic use. US data processing.
